growth_records.lvlwhy: species-level trait compilation (Madin) — no strain recorded in source
growth_records.cite: Madin JS et al. A synthesis of bacterial and archaeal phenotypic trait data. Sci Data 7:170 (2020); source dataset: patric.
growth_records.notes: Species-level trait record; culture mode/pH often unspecified; medium + uptake/secretion pending literature curation.

growth_records.cite: Aoyagi, Luciano N. et al. Diversity and characterization of the ammonia-oxidizing bacteria responsible for nitrification in tea field soils. Scientific Reports (2025). DOI:10.1038/s41598-025-18596-5. [PMC12475048]
growth_records.snippet: At their optimal pH, the µ values were similar for TNL100 (0.31 ± 0.01 d−1) and TNL300 (0.34 ± 0.01 d−1) ... The K_m values for ammonium oxidation by ... TNL100 ... were 0.85 ... mM.
growth_records.notes: Chemolithoautotrophic beta-AOB Nitrosospira sp. (OTU2; high similarity to N. multiformis). Reported mu=0.31 d-1 at optimal pH 7.5, from log-linear slope of nitrite production during exponential phase (days 2-8). CONVERSIONS: mu_per_h = 0.31/24 = 0.01292 h-1; doubling_time = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.31 d = 2.236 d = 53.66 h. Substrate=ammonium, product=nitrite; no quantified mmol/gDW/h rate reported. Km (ammonium oxidation) = 0.85 mM (NH4+ + NH3) via ammonium-dependent O2 uptake (Suppl Fig S7). Tolerated up to 100 mM ammonium, fastest growth 40-80 mM. Base medium composition cited from ref 106 (not stated); temp 25 C, 110 rpm.

growth_records.cite: Aoyagi, Luciano N. et al. Diversity and characterization of the ammonia-oxidizing bacteria responsible for nitrification in tea field soils. Scientific Reports (2025). DOI:10.1038/s41598-025-18596-5. [PMC12475048]
growth_records.snippet: whereas TNL200 exhibited slower growth (0.20 ± 0.02 d−1) (Fig 3d, h, and f, respectively). ... The K_m values for ammonium oxidation by ... TNL200 ... were ... 0.39 ... mM.
growth_records.notes: Chemolithoautotrophic beta-AOB Nitrosospira sp. (OTU5; clusters with Nitrosospira sp. 56-18, 97.07% identity). Reported mu=0.20 d-1 at optimal pH 7.5, from log-linear slope of nitrite production during exponential phase (days 3-9). CONVERSIONS: mu_per_h = 0.20/24 = 0.008333 h-1; doubling_time = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.20 d = 3.466 d = 83.18 h. Substrate=ammonium, product=nitrite; no quantified mmol/gDW/h rate reported. Km (ammonium oxidation) = 0.39 mM (NH4+ + NH3), the lowest of the four isolates, via ammonium-dependent O2 uptake. Tolerated only up to 20 mM ammonium (slower growth above). Base medium composition cited from ref 106 (not stated); temp 25 C, 110 rpm.

growth_records.cite: Aoyagi, Luciano N. et al. Diversity and characterization of the ammonia-oxidizing bacteria responsible for nitrification in tea field soils. Scientific Reports (2025). DOI:10.1038/s41598-025-18596-5. [PMC12475048]
growth_records.snippet: At their optimal pH, the µ values were similar for TNL100 (0.31 ± 0.01 d−1) and TNL300 (0.34 ± 0.01 d−1) ... The K_m values for ammonium oxidation by ... TNL300 ... were ... 0.99 mM.
growth_records.notes: Chemolithoautotrophic beta-AOB Nitrosospira sp. (OTU2 with TNL100; ANI ~100% to TNL100, same species; high similarity to N. multiformis). Reported mu=0.34 d-1 at optimal pH 7.5, from log-linear slope of nitrite production during exponential phase (days 1-5). CONVERSIONS: mu_per_h = 0.34/24 = 0.014167 h-1; doubling_time = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.34 d = 2.038 d = 48.92 h. Substrate=ammonium, product=nitrite; no quantified mmol/gDW/h rate reported. Km (ammonium oxidation) = 0.99 mM (NH4+ + NH3) via ammonium-dependent O2 uptake. Tolerated up to 100 mM ammonium, fastest growth 40-80 mM. Base medium composition cited from ref 106 (not stated); temp 25 C, 110 rpm.
